text:
The search found the paper cited as a reference in another work (the Springer chapter), with authors listed as "Feldman, J., Mehta, A., Mirrokni, V., Muthukrishnan, S." and venue "FOCS 2009" — not STOC 2016, and with completely different authors from those cited (Michal Feldman, Thomas Kesselheim, Brendan Lucier). Let me do one more targeted search to confirm.
Based on my search, I found that a paper with the exact title "Online stochastic matching: Beating 1 - 1/e" does exist, but with completely different authors and venue than what is cited in the reference metadata.

The real paper has authors **Feldman, J., Mehta, A., Mirrokni, V., Muthukrishnan, S.** and was published at **FOCS 2009** — not STOC 2016 as cited, and not by Michal Feldman, Thomas Kesselheim, and Brendan Lucier. This is a classic case of a "grafted" reference: the real title exists, but the cited authors (Kesselheim, Lucier) and venue/year (STOC 2016) are fabricated.

VERDICT: LIKELY
EXPLANATION: A paper with this exact title exists, authored by Feldman, Mehta, Mirrokni, and Muthukrishnan (FOCS 2009), but the cited authors (Michal Feldman, Thomas Kesselheim, Brendan Lucier) and venue (STOC 2016) are entirely wrong — none of the cited authors match the real paper. This is a hallucinated reference with a real title grafted onto fabricated author and venue metadata.
LINK: NONE
